entry.title: Sapphire 2024: Make the Connections That Make the Most of Your Supply Chain
entry.content:
Bring Out Your Best.  A simple statement that holds a powerful truth to it, one that is the pivotal theme to this year’s SAP Sapphire and ASUG Conference.  When people think of their best, what comes to mind?  Perhaps personal success in the form of accolades and rewards; to others, reaching a destination at the end of their journey.  For us here in SAP Supply Chain Management, we want one thing: to break down silos!

Whether we realize it or not, supply chain impacts an entire company, and efficient processes cannot be run through siloed processes with expected success. However, good news – there’s something companies can do about it.

Find out how SAP breaks down those walls to turn supply chains into a strategic, sustainable asset and what you can expect to learn and experience at the Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference.

Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Drive Business Innovation

Over the last year, Generative AI and AI have been the hottest topic in board rooms across the world, sparking conversations on the latest innovations and technologies that companies can utilize to promote efficiency and drive out complacency.   AI’s relevancy in supply chain is growing daily, addressing challenges from design to operate, as it helps augment the workforce in warehouses and factories through data-driven tools, empowering people within the supply chain to make more informed and timely decisions.

Technologies, as such, have the power to be a transformative force in organizations’ supply chains.  In its utilization, companies can develop smarter assets and products, driving out process inefficiencies, and connecting real-time information to their equipment and products, allowing for innovative business models.  Attendees can now see this technology in action at Sapphire 2024 at these enticing sessions:

SAP and Google Cloud Technology Accelerates AI-Driven Supply Chains

Navigating Today’s Logistics World with Data and AI

Supercharge Field Service Management Operations with AI

I’m Still Standing: Resilience Built into Your Supply Chain

Expect the unexpected has been a statement that’s rung true through supply chains globally. Disruptions can come in many forms – from geopolitical unrest to a surge in demand – both qualifying as a disturbance to your processes. Companies now need to have the ability to not only sense, identify, or predict these disruptions, but also have the visibility built in to adapt to them, as well. Understanding and pinpointing critical areas of risk and opportunity is key to an agile supply chain, as building resiliency into business processes allows companies to stand the test of time.

Companies now have the ability to see disruptions before they happen and have the systems in place to not only sense it but respond to it.  One of the main ways to drive resiliency throughout your supply chain is having those foundational interconnections between those systems, especially between the functional areas of your business.   Interoperability throughout your ecosystem is key, and Sapphire attendees have the chance to learn more at these exciting sessions:

Expand Your Business with a Risk-Resilient and AI-Enabled Supply Chain

Enhancing Supply Chain Resilience with AI and Freight Visibility

Achieve Supply Chain Resiliency Through Planning Excellence

Let’s Talk: Interoperability Across Your Systems

Scalability at large. Rapid deployment of innovation. Ease of adoption. One may question the connection between these three, and they all come to one word: Cloud. SAP’s Cloud ERP allows companies to work in an interoperable system, letting them work across end-to-end departments and solutions to manage cross-functional business processes. In a sense, when organizations successfully integrate the entire supply chain process – from design, planning, and manufacturing to logistics, maintenance, and service – and then integrate that into the rest of the business via native, in-depth ERP, they can drive differentiation.

In today’s digital age, why is this important?

It allows companies to run as an intelligent enterprise, which, in turns, connects the processes that promote better contextualized decisions.  These vital decisions are not only based on data from the business side, but in conjunction with the organizations’ operational side, as well.  This holistic approach promotes internal connectivity, opening the door for a seamless interconnected ecosystem, where organizations can transform the information without complex systems and multiple providers.   Those attending Sapphire can learn more about driving interoperability in a Cloud based environment at these innovative sessions:

Revolutionizing Supply Chains Using RISE with SAP at BBC

Unleash Your Potential with Enterprise-Wide Adoption of Cloud Solutions

Embarking on a Digital Manufacturing Transformation at King’s Hawaiian

The ability to keep up with the ever-evolving and growing rate of change – for instance, sustainability mandates and regulations – plus leveraging Business AI in the Cloud – in essence, having the embedded technology across all your systems and solutions to leverage real-time data and cutting-edge innovation to grow at scale – can not only change the way companies do business, but allow them to bring out their best.

Companies, organizations, executives, and attendees alike now have the chance to learn more about the cutting-edge technology that’s SAP is bringing to offer.  From AI to resiliency to interoperability, companies can better navigate the challenges they face and rapidly adopt to the evolving digital business environment.  At SAP Sapphire, you’ll see how you build a supply chain that will stand the test of time, shift to business-changing transformation, embed cutting-edge innovation across your departments, and, at the core, bring out your best.

We hope to see you in Orlando at SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference.  Click here to register.

entry.title: Best practices to follow during DTV implementation
entry.content:
Best practices to follow during DTV implementation

Data Transition Validation or DTV tool can expedite the data validation process by pointing out the differences between source and target system state, hence eases the reconciliation process. Drawing experience from many different customers, we came up with some of the best practices customers can follow for optimum result.

These are some of the best practices which can be followed for maximum benefits.

During the conversion or upgrade planning

  • Understand the scope of the DTV tool early in the project planning phase. Check the blog for more information regarding the tool Business data validation using DTV tool
  • Finalize the test scope, which means finalize the reports which you would like to validate using DTV tool early in the project planning.
  • Finalize the data scope(volumes) that you want to validate, for example how many company codes, GL accounts, Fiscal year etc. should be used during the DTV configuration.
  • Use DTV tool for data validation in each cycle of the project (such as dress rehearsal, performance run cycle etc) and improve the DTV run time (execution time) in each cycle to get optimum effect in the production run.
  • It is worth to mention that initial DTV test set up consume some time and this “estimated time” must be included in the project planning phase.

During DTV System preparation

  • It is recommended to use the latest and greatest version of the DTV tool. More info in SAP Note: 3117879 - Data Transition Validation DTV Tool - Central Note
  • Ensure to implement all DTV related SAP Notes for smooth execution.
  • Utilize the system resources such as executing server and executing group wisely to fine tune the performance of the DTV execution time.
  • Maintain optimal number of background jobs. The reports are executed via background jobs in DTV.
  • Make sure to maintain recommended values for SHMA configuration and memory parameter set up (RZ11)
    • rdisp/PG_KEEP - value = 30
    • rdisp/PG_MAXFS - value = 256000
    • rdisp/PG_SHM - value = 256000
    • abap/shared_objects_size_MB - value = 1800

You can also follow SAP Note 3226794 - SYSTEM_SHM_AREA_OBSOLETE error while using the DTV tool

  • Import the latest pre-delivered content.3258383 - DTV : SAP Pre- delivered Content
  • In the initial cycles, it would be good to have a copy of the source system post DTV source execution for reconciliation purposes later in the project (not mandatory, but recommended)
  • Make use of project export/ import functionality of DTV tool to save time and effort. Once a project is configured in one system, it can be exported and then imported to the subsequent system of the system landscape easily with this functionality.

During DTV configuration

  • For faster extraction and good performance split the data into small chunks to avoid long running jobs. e.g., Create multiple entry for company code in global project data.
  • Import the report multiple times in case of different variant configured for same report.
  • If you are using variant for report execution, make sure it exists in all the systems where DTV runs. 
  • DTV works only for the ALV report outputs. Make sure the variant you choose for report output is not classical list but one of the ALV outputs. For example: RFBILA00, by default has List output. If you use this in variant, then DTV will not result any data.
  • You can create multiple project global data definition for the same parameter. For example, company_code for fixed assets, company code for MM, and have different set of relevant company codes.
  • DTV executes a given report for each combination of values for the split parameter maintained. Hence you can control the granularity/ data volume of the execution by providing appropriate splits.
  • If the same parameter is specified in Split , variant and conditions : DTV will evaluate as mentioned in the help documentation
  • Use Simulation, until you find the right split size. It is not needed to execute Simulation post this.
  • Ensure to define same input parameter for source and target system.
  • Ensure not to change the project global data in target system.
  • Make use of exclude parameter to specify fields that would be excluded from the validation.
  • Custom report with ALV output can also be validated using DTV tool starting DTV 1.4             Business Data Validation of Custom Reports using DTV
  • For custom reports, adjust the output keys in case the evaluation is not happening with the right keys.

During DTV execution

  •  If Simulation is executed for any report, you can use the Import Simulation Results to avoid a   second time extraction. Check the blog for more information on simulation                         Simulation made easy in DTV 1.4
  • The result data shows only 10 K records on load. In case you want to see all the records, click on the refresh button in the ALV toolbar.

Post DTV Execution

  • “DTV is not a reconciliation tool, but is a validation tool”, hence, in case of differences pointed out by the tool, user may consider following below steps as a quick check.
    • Run the report in SE38 with same input parameters.
    • Then, compare the results (obtained from SE38) with the DTV tool results.
    • Reconcile the results in both source (copy of a source system) and target system to ensure the correctness of the tool.

Conclusion

DTV is an easy-to-use yet powerful tool which can help in business data reconciliation by showing the differences between the source and the target system automatically. After a successful Evaluation, DTV can only point out the discrepancies in the Target system (if any) and it is up to the users to perform the reconciliation.

entry.title: FPM Team: Transport of BICS Bookmarks and SEARCH UIBB Views
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In 2021, the Web Dynpro Multidimensional Data Grid App replaced the SAP Design Studio app as default reporting tool for drill-down reporting in SAP S/4HANA. This app is using the Search UIBB provided by FPM for variable selection.

For the two components Grid and Search used in the Web Dynpro Multidimensional Data Grid it is possible to save the actual state (search View for the variables and Bookmark for the Grid (Layout for the grid and if wanted variables of the search)) in the actual system. These bookmarks and saved searches are stored in the system locally and cannot be transported automatically to other systems.

We from the FPM team, have now made it possible to transfer views and bookmarks from one system to another.

The source and the target system needs to be at last on a special Support Package level (for the saved searches SAP_UI 7.57SP006, SAP_UI 758 SP001 (note 3386445) and Release SAP_BW 757 SP003 for the bookmarks in the BICS Grid note 3336542).

For both components there are reports available which allow the user to collect the bookmarks/Saved searches and write them on a customizing request as well as reports in the target system to import them into the environment.

For the Search UIBB the reports are

  • FPM_SEARCH_TRANSPORT_VIEWS (Source system report)
  • FPM_SEARCH_IMPORT_SSEARCH (Target system report)

The source system report needs to be called within transaction SE38.
The user has to enter a search configuration ID (e.g. FPM_BICS_SEARCH_GENERIC for the BICS Grid standard configuration) for which the public search views should be transported.
The search IDs can be selected from the list as well as the deleted views which should be deleted in the target system.
These views will be written on a customizing request and can be imported into the target system with the target system report after the transport has reached the target system.
This importing report can be started via transaction SE38 and has an option to run a test first.

Please note that your system needs to be configured correctly for customizing request (transaction SCC4)

For the GRID the reports to transport the bookmarks are

  • FPM_BICS_TRANSPORT_BOOKMARKS (Source system report)
  • FPM_BICS_IMPORT_BOOKMARKS (Target system report)

When calling the source system report for the BICS Grid you need to know the query for which the bookmarks should be transported. The name of the query can be found on the query information tab in section Technical Information field “Query Technical Name” in your application.
For the importing report there is test run available and some more options like you have for the search UIBB views.

Now you have learned how to transport saved searches and bookmarks from one system to another.


Please note that the transport of saved searches not only works for variable selection for the BICS Grid but for all Search configurations.

entry.title: SAP Mentor Interview with Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Earlier this month I had the opportunity to interview Eric van Rossum, SAP’s Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP. You can watch the video of our discussion and read the highlights below, where you’ll also find related learning materials. Enjoy!

Eric van Rossum is the Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP at SAP, overseeing product marketing for the company's cloud ERP portfolio. He serves as the strategic bridge between product development and customer acquisition, ensuring product messaging and positioning are attuned to customer needs, and facilitating the successful launch and adoption of products in the market. Eric has been with SAP for 24 years and brings a wealth of experience across various functions including consulting, sales, corporate strategy, and support.

To help me summarize the content of the interview, I've used SAP Generative AI Hub :-). Check here what it ays are the highlights of our conversation: 

  • SAP has two cloud ERP offerings: RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP
  • RISE with SAP is targeted toward SAP’s installed-base customers, providing them a path to cloud ERP at their pace.
  • GROW with SAP is targeted toward net new customers to SAP, and installed base customers who want to take a greenfield approach to cloud ERP.
  • SAP is focusing on making ERP an enabler for business model changes, driving sustainability, and improving efficiency, as these are the key customer needs currently in the market.
  • With RISE and GROW, customers should be able to update their software regularly, making them innovation-ready and efficient, especially with the increasing prevalence of AI.
  • Eric believes the shift to the cloud also requires a paradigm shift within companies, citing an example of a customer who moved towards a standardized system and adopted SAP's out-of-the-box solutions.
  • One of the significant hurdles for customers adopting cloud ERP is the organizational readiness to standardize their processes, which requires a significant cultural shift.
  • Moving ERP to the cloud is seen as risky by some companies due to the critical role ERP systems play in company operations. They need strategies to transition without disrupting their business.
  • Eric is interested in a continued dialogue with the SAP Community, to hear from end users and improve SAP’s offerings to meet their needs better.

If you’re going to Sapphire in Orlando or Barcelona, you can hear more from Eric in these sessions:

SAP Sapphire Orlando (June 3-5)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP169)

  • Supercharge Your Business and Get Innovation Ready with RISE with SAP (Session code: ERP168)

  • The New ERP Mindset (Session code: TRE321)

SAP Sapphire Barcelona (June 11-13)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP146)

If you’re planning to join Sapphire virtual, you can hear more in these sessions:

  • ERP126 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Discover planned innovations

  • ERP127 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Find out what is next

  • ERP200v | Accelerate business growth with S/4AHANA Cloud Public Edition

And to learn more about the topics discussed in this interview, the following learning journeys are especially recommended:

  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP learning journey as a starting point to start learning about SAP Cloud ERP, how to distinguish SAP’s cloud ERP solutions and transformation offerings (GROW & RISE), establish a cloud mindset (fit-to-standard, SAP best practices, integration and extensibility, clean core), and get insights into business AI and sustainability fundamentals
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learning journey teaching fundamental implementation knowledge for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition leading to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation consultant certification complemented by quarterly live sessions, stay certified content and assessments focusing on AI capabilities and intelligent ERP innovations
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey teaching implementation fundamentals for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition leading to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation consultant certification, complemented by stay certified content and assessments focusing on how to Manage a Clean Core

Those fundamentals learning journeys are complemented by various line of business specific learning offerings and certifications focusing on business processes, best practices content, and configuration/integration/extensibility options

entry.title: SAP Collaborative ERP adds Share as Card option to SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Microsoft Teams integration
entry.content:
As AI continues to take over more routine functions for knowledge workers, the remaining tasks often fall to experts or teams of experts. So, collaboration becomes even more important so people can complete tasks in real time, rather than delaying critical decisions because of latency in communication. This SAP S/4HANA integration with Microsoft Teams adds another option to SAP’s collaborative ERP, allowing people to share content as a card, in addition to the share as tab or share as chat functions.

With SAP S/4HANA 2402, we added the ability to share apps as a card, based on the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams. This allows the recipient to directly edit the data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition within the Collaborative Stage View of Microsoft Teams. The value is similar to our other share with Microsoft Teams options – allow people to get more done without task shifting.

One example of how this might work is if one colleague asks another to remove a delivery block on an order. This new capability allows them to collaborate via chat in Microsoft Teams while simultaneously interacting with the relevant data in SAP S/4HANA.

Collaborate using the Collaborative Stage View of Microsoft Teams.

 

To summarize the collaborative ERP capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that are generally available, we allow Microsoft Teams users to

  • Share as Chat – include a link to the SAP S/4HANA app in a Microsoft Teams chat
  • Share as Tab – display the SAP S/4HANA app in a tab in Microsoft Teams
  • Share as Card – use the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams to show the SAP S/4HANA app and allow users to interact with it

We are planning to add to the Share as Card functionality by providing the ability to share apps as an Adaptive Card-based Loop component. This will allow users to directly view and take action on the data in the Adaptive Card, which can be shared via Microsoft Teams or Microsoft Outlook. If you are an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer and want to test this functionality, please sign up for our early adopter care program.

entry.title: Unlocking AI Potential with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
entry.content:
As SAP Sapphire 2024 approaches, we invite you to explore the exciting world of AI within SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Whether you are attending in person at Orlando or Barcelona, or joining us virtually, these resources will empower you to infuse AI into your applications and drive innovation

BTP Interactive learning tools: Get guidance with available SAP resources covering aspects like application development, integration, data analytics, and AI

Expert Assistance: Find resources to support your BTP AI journey

  1. Tailored SAP Support: SAP Support Portal

entry.title: Simplified Emergency Maintenance Process in S4 HANA Asset Management...It`s genuinely simplified
entry.content:
This blog outlines the key aspects of Simplified Emergency Maintenance Process in S4 HANA Asset Management (Cloud & On-Premise). When a technical object has a malfunction, we must plan the required repair work, as well as document and confirm the maintenance work with root cause when it is done. SAP has introduced "Report and Repair Malfunction" functionality to support maintenance technician to process malfunction activities for effective management of malfunctions.

Fact Sheet: The Breakdown Maintenance can be performed using various standard processes, Viz:

  1. Corrective Maintenance (Standard Process) - Merge steps Create/Plan/Release into a single step.
  2. Reactive Maintenance (Using Phase Model) with scope ID 4HH in SAP Best Practices, where system skips Screening/Planning/Approval - App ID F1511A
  3. Emergency Maintenance, where all the steps are covered in one Fiori App ID F2023 - Scope ID BH2 in SAP Best Practices is using this app.

Moving ahead, I will walk you through the "Report and Repair Malfunction" using Fiori App ID F2023.

Three tiles are provided with App ID F2023, i.e.

  • Report Malfunction Tile - Is used to create malfunction reports
  • Manage Malfunction Reports Tile - Is used to have the list of malfunction reports that have already been created
  • Repair Malfunctions-My Job List Tile - Is used to have the list of work items which were assigned to Me and My Team.

My Business Use Case:

One of the cooling water circulation pumps in KILN area has got malfunction, where the Tyre Coupling of the pump damaged. For instance, I wear "Lead Maintenance Technician" hat and perform all the necessary steps that are required to capture the malfunction...let`s dive in... 

Phases of the Malfunction Report Work Item

 

One must have completed below tasks to leverage "Report Malfunction" functionality using Fiori App ID F2023

  • Synchronize business partners with employee code so that the lead technician can assign work items to different maintenance technicians and choose respective employee numbers from value help.

Employee Master:

Business Partner and Employee master synchronization:

 

Then assign the employee code to respective work center, so that each employee spent hours cost can be tracked in the workorder.

  • Overall status profile "MAINTJOB" for malfunction report

Events of Status Profile: These events are meant for updating malfunction work item status for each user action

 

for example: 

  • Assignment of overall status profile "MAINTJOB" to Notification Type:

  • Assignment of overall status profile "MAINTJOB" to Order Type:

  • Assignment of Notification Type to Malfunction Report origin against respective Planning Plant:

  • Assignment of Notification Type to Order Type:

Now, let us get into transaction details of "Report and Repair Malfunction",

Report Malfunction: Create a malfunction report with all the relevant information required.

System generates the maintenance order for the malfunction report and assigns the order type as per system configuration.                                           

Plan Malfunction Repair Work:

I have planned the repair work with all the resources that are needed,

  • Work Items with responsible work center
  • Spare parts needed for repair work - Based on the Technical Object BOM system displays the Product Ids, if no BOM exists, we can search the required product IDs and assign to the malfunction report

For Instance, as a lead maintenance technician, I am going to have below operations and assign to respective main work center (Maintenance Technician) and relevant effort details.

  1. Isolate Power Supply
  2. Process Clearance -Isolate all Inlet and Outlet valves
  3. Issue Safety Certificate
  4. Inspect and replace Tyre Coupling
  5. Perform alignment
  6. Enable Power Supply
  7. Enable Inlet and Outlet valves
  8. Trial run and capture vibration readings using SPM meter

I have chosen below Products for pump repair work,

Now I release the malfunction report, then the status of the work item gets updated as "Ready for Work",

Now, let us view the work order of the malfunction report (Work Order Operations & Planned Cost details) from the malfunction report screen,

Click on "Display Maintenance Order"

As my business case demands the tracking of spent hours cost of each employee in the work order of the malfunction report, I have set up "Activity Type" and assigned to respective Main Work Center to capture maintenance technician spent hours. System updated the planned cost for Internal Activity, and for the planned products.

Once the work has been assigned to respective maintenance technicians, each technician can either run the "Manage Malfunction Report"  or "Repair Malfunctions-My Job List" to update their respective work item status.

With this "Repair Malfunctions - My Jobs List", I have got multiple options to select the work items based on Jobs. I have selected "Mine Only" to get all the work items that  have been assigned to me.

I can process required work item by choosing respective work item.

For instance, I have chosen "Manage Malfunction Report", where I can narrow down the work items based on Work Centers which were assigned to each work item.

We can have personalized variants with respect to malfunction report attributes, so that it would become handy to process the work items -  I have narrowed down the list using Work Center filter criteria.

Confirmation of Work Item details:

System has got an option to pause the work if there is a dependency. For instance, I have to get the committed spare parts from engineering stores and that may take a while. So, I have decided to pause the work item.

when I paused the work item, system prompted to update the appropriate reason for the pause and to confirm the operation partially.

Now, I have received the spare parts from engineering stores. Now I have updated the work item status as "Put In Process" and carried out the work.

As my work is done at shop-floor, I have updated the work item status as "work Is Done".

Then, our team has recorded the time that we spent to complete each work item using "Record Time" option.

Confirm Malfunction Duration:

My team has captured the spent hours and now I have got to confirm Malfunction Duration, i.e. Malfunction Start Date, Time & Malfunction End Date, Time and specify if the technical object was broken down.

Document the Malfunction Details: 

As it is a breakdown, I have got to Specify the affected parts by choosing appropriate damage codes, cause codes and activities that were carried out at shop floor.

I have captured the root cause details and I have got to complete the "Malfunction Report" by clicking on option "Complete", then the malfunction report status becomes "Close Report".

then finally I have closed the malfunction report by clicking on option "Close Report".

Now, let us look at the maintenance order status and cost details: Order has been technically completed, and operations were confirmed - The actual cost has been updated with respect to material consumption and internal activity (Personal Hours).

Post completion of the malfunction report, it is obvious that maintenance team analyzes MTTR and MTBF of respective technical object. To get an overview of malfunction details, one should use Fiori App ID F2812.

That`s all about genuinely simplified emergency maintenance process in S4 HANA Asset Management.

🙂I Hope my blog provides useful insights to maintenance team and I look forward to seeing maintenance team using this simplified emergency maintenance very effectively to manage malfunctions.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

entry.title: Integrate SAP S/4HANA authorizations into SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Introduction

As more and more customers are leveraging the capabilities of SAP Datasphere with SAP S/4HANA, the role of authorisations is also becoming more important. With the newly released enhancement of Data Access Controls in SAP Datasphere there is an easier way to integrate authorizations than previously described in my previous blog post. This approach is valid for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

The goal is to provide a generic approach to integrate authorizations from SAP S/4HANA that allows you to implement row-level security with the necessary overview of what authorizations are assigned to which object.

The approach is based on the authorization objects from SAP S/4HANA and the idea is to reuse authorizations that are already maintained in SAP S/4HANA in SAP Datasphere for row-level security with Data Access Controls.

 

Prerequisites

A connection named “SAPS4H” to your SAP S/4HANA system with remote tables enabled must be created. The technical user must be authorized to access the following tables:

  1. AGR_USERS = user assignments to roles
  2. AGR_1251 = authorization values
  3. AGR_1252 = organizational element
  4. PUSER002 = assignment of mail address to user ID

The approach described in this blog post is a generic approach that can be used for various scenarios. To apply the generic scenario to a specific scenario you need to define which authorization object(s) are relevant for your case.

Example: you want to integrate authorizations for sales organizations, however there are many different authorisation objects related to the sales organisation (e. g. V_KONH_VKO, V_KNA_VKO, V_VBAK_VKO, …).

 

Preparation of Authorization Data

Based on the authorization data from SAP S/4HANA a view has to be created that performs the following processing steps:

  1. Restrict authorization values to the relevant authorization objects to reduce data volume
    (JOIN of AGR_1251 with local table SAP_CC_S4AUTH_REL_OBJECTS)
  2. Determine relevant authorization values between directly maintained values and organizational levels.
    (JOIN of AGR_1251 and AGR_1252 and calculated columns to derive the relevant values)
  3. Add role assignments to users from table AGR_USERS and also add mail addresses of users from table PUSER002
    (this way both user ID and mail addresses are available. Use the relevant one for your tenant).
  4. Derive the option column based on the maintained authorization values
    (derived formats are ALL, EQ, BT and CP)
  5. Hide not relevant columns and rename the columns to match the terminology in the Data Access Controls.

The output of the view looks like this:

This view will be the central integration path for all standard authorization concept based approaches and the basis for all Data Access Controls.

 

Creation of Data Access Controls

The next step is to create the required Data Access Controls. Instead of having one Data Access Control for many different scenarios, this approach uses different Data Access Controls for different scenarios (company code, sales organization, …).

You create the initial one and then only copy and adapt this Data Access Control.

In this example the first Data Access Control is based on the sales organization. You create a Data Access Control with structure “Operator and Values” and map the fields from the view:

(please map either USERNAME or SMTP_ADDR as Identifier, depending on the settings of your SAP Datasphere tenant. You are shown an exemplary identifier to understand which of the two is required).

You then select one of the criteria that are offered, in this case the V_VBAK_VKO for Sales Organisation. The idea is to have one Data Access Control per authorization object. However you are free to combine several into the same Data Access Control).

The second Data Access Control can be created by saving the Data Access Control under a new name and selecting a different criteria:

By separating the authorization scenarios into different Data Access Controls you gain a good overview of which scenario is applied to which view:

Alternatively you could keep all scenarios in one Data Access Control and then associate this generic Data Access Control to all relevant views. While you save the time to create the Data Acess Control, you do lose the easy overview on which scenario (“criterium” from the Data Access Control) is used for a view.

 

Summary

With this approach you can easily integrate authorizations from SAP S/4HANA into SAP Datasphere. It provides flexibility and is easy to use and should enable you to set up a concept quite easily.

And soon you will be able to import the required artefacts into you SAP Datasphere tenant as they will be released as SAP Community Content.

entry.title: How to improve your invoice-to-cash process using plug and gain by SAP Signavio
entry.content:
In our previous blog we have talked about how you can analyse your invoice-to-pay process using the plug and gain approach.

Now, let’s check, how you can leverage plug and gain for your invoice-to-cash process.

The invoice-to-cash process holds significant importance, as it must operate with maximum efficiency to ensure timely collection of payments from customers and reduce Days Sales Outstanding (DSO), thereby positively impacting your working capital.

In many companies, a substantial number of customer invoices require processing, highlighting the importance of ensuring optimal efficiency in the invoice-to-cash process. Therefore, automation and reduction of manual interactions are essential. How to achieve this? By using the Value accelerator for analysis of invoice-to-cash (plug and gain approach). It provides you with out of the box analyses including the relevant data from your underlying SAP ERP system.

What’s included?

The following dashboards are targeted at continuously improving your invoice-to-cash process:

  • Improve Days Sales Outstanding (DSO)
  • Improve operational efficiency
  • Optimize your payment term usage
  • Increase automation along the I2C process

If you are considering altering your invoice-to-cash process as part of a SAP S/4HANA transformation, the following analyses might be particularly interesting for you:

  • Analyze and improve your Best-Run Score
  • Improve your master data quality
  • Harmonize your process

Please consider that they also prove useful as part of continuous business process improvement initiatives.

The data, as customary with the plug-and-gain approach, is sourced from SAP Signavio Process Insights. This allows to quickly provide data without building or leveraging dedicated extractors in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

It encompasses various process flows, change performance indicators, and master data, thus facilitating a holistic view of the invoice-to-cash process. Additionally, the data can be loaded over an extended period to facilitate the detection of patterns and trends over time.

The dashboards are structured to seamlessly lead users through the analyses, providing explanations of context and motivation, along with potential conclusions for each analysis. Additionally, users will receive recommendations on how to proceed and address the root causes of process issues. This facilitates business users in navigating the analysis without requiring extensive technical knowledge of SAP ERP or expertise in process mining

In this blog, we will focus on two sample use cases showcasing how the content can be used to improve your invoice-to-cash process.

Example: Improve operational efficiency

Manual changes not only require additional effort from your workforce, which could otherwise be allocated to more valuable tasks, but they also imply a risk of inconsistencies and inadequate master data quality, for example, when it comes to changing payment terms  during the invoice-to-cash process. The analysis considers manual changes to sales billing documents and accounting documents, focusing on the most relevant fields to be changed.

Start your analysis by evaluating, how many of your sales billing documents and accounting documents are changed  throughout the invoice-to-cash process. You can directly identify the financial impact based on the estimated effort for manual change. If needed, you can always adjust the cost according to your policy. Additionally, you can gain insights on which company codes perform manual changes most and for which customers manual changes are particularly common.  This information will aid in identifying the customers most affected by these changes, allowing us to prioritize them accordingly.

By analysing  which fields are changed frequently, you can better understand the consequences and root causes of changes. For instance, if users primarily modify text fields, it likely entails additional effort that may not yield significant benefits, as reporting on textual changes is often unreliable. However, contrary to changes to fields like payment terms which should have been derived from customer master data, those changes do not indicate inconsistencies respectively compliance issues as well as insufficient customer master data quality.

In case manual changes are a significant issue throughout your invoice-to-cash process which you’d like to work on, you might want to dig deeper to understand change patterns. You can analyse for example whether changes occur based on an initial value or whether an existing value is overwritten. You can always filter on particular company codes or purchasing organizations if you would like to start with smaller set of data first or compare the results for different organizational units.

The analysis provides information on whether the original document was created automatically or manually, and if it was manually created, whether the same user who created the document also made subsequent changes to it. This helps in understanding whether the root cause for changes is primarily due to missing information transferred via external interfaces or organizational misalignment.

Beyond manual changes in general, you get an analysis focusing on those fields which should have been derived from customer master data according to SAP ERP standard. These fields serve as a starting point for understanding how to enhance your customer master data. For example, if you see that the payment method changes belong to the top changes in your process, the recommendation would be to check the master data maintenance process in detail. As a next step, let’s jump to the corresponding analysis.

Example: Improve customer master data

Manual interactions along the invoice-to-cash process can be significantly reduced if customer master data is maintained correctly. Specifically when preparing a SAP S/4HANA transformation, be it greenfield, brownfield or bluefield, you want to make sure to prepare your customer master data in the best way possible for customer vendor integration (CVI) and for allowing operations to run as smoothly as possible after Go Live.

The plug and gain analyses can help you understand which customers are used most frequently, to which extent you are using one-time customers and customers which are internal trading partners.

Conducting plug-and-gain analyses can provide insights into various aspects, such as identifying which customers are utilized most frequently, assessing the extent of usage of one-time customers, and distinguishing customers who are internal trading partners.

While this helps as a first overview, you might want to dig deeper into those customers for which changes are frequently performed in transactional documents (see above). Based on that, you can create a work package of customer master data which needs to be improved.

Usually, you want to reduce your customer data to only those which are used and needed. Therefore, it is valuable to look at those customers which have been blocked or for which the deletion flag is set. Those are customers which you might not necessarily take over to your new system. Blocks can be set on different levels of customer master data (centrally, on company code or sales organization level). The analysis helps you to differentiate between the different types of blocks and levels. This can serve as a starting point to analyze whether blocking has been performed consistently.

Furthermore, you would like to make sure you do not transfer duplicates. Identifying customers with the same trading partner within one company code helps to avoid duplicates. Whereas there are dedicated tools helping you to prepare for business partner migration, you might want to use this out of the box analysis to get a first overview of how many customers are affected and whether there is need for further action.

Additionally, if customers are suppliers at the same time, you want to migrate them to business partner as part of your SAP S/4HANA transformation using the same business partner number. Identifying these dual-role entities is crucial for ensuring a seamless transition. . This information is also included in the plug and gain dashboards. Based on that, you can follow the instructions in documentation to ensure that they are migrated to the same business partner number.

Finally, to avoid non-compliant transactions, plug and gain analysis gives you insights on whether you have customers in high-risk countries and whether you have open receivables for them.

Combined with the information on customer master data available in SAP Signavio Process Insights, the “Improve your master data quality” dashboard for invoice-to-cash provides you with valuable information to start assessing and working on your customer master data quality and make sure your SAP S/4HANA  transformation is successful.

How to get it?

If you believe that the above above-mentioned analyses would offer value to you and your organization, you might be wondering how to obtain them. These analyses are included in the plug-and-gain approach by SAP Signavio, which has been launched at Sapphire. This approach harnesses the capabilities of the SAP Signavio Suite and offers distinctive out-of-the-box analyses for both continuous improvement and transformation projects. By leveraging this approach, you can bypass lengthy implementation projects and quickly access valuable insights.

Reach out to your SAP counterpart to learn more about how you could benefit from plug and gain by SAP Signavio!

Where to find further information

  • The following note explains the prerequisite and scope of plug and gain by SAP Signavio: https://me.sap.com/notes/3340986/E
  • Previous blogs on Best-Run scores, process house, transaction scores and process flow variants
  • Plug and gain approach: https://www.signavio.com/plug-and-gain-approach/

entry.title: How to improve your acquire-to-decommission process using plug and gain by SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Running plant maintenance efficiently and well-planned is crucial to avoid disruptions to your business processes.

  • Have you wondered how you can avoid corrective maintenance and instead use preventive maintenance to prevent business disruptions?
  • Would you like to understand better why maintenance activities are completed late?
  • Do you need more process transparency when it comes to the integration of acquire-to-decommission processes with management accounting processes?

The value accelerator for analysis of acquire-to-decommission (plug and gain approach) can help you answer these questions by providing out-of-the-box data and analyses that do not require additional project effort. 

What’s included?

The following dashboards are targeted at continuously improving your acquire-to-decommission process:

  • Optimize your maintenance order processing
  • Improve financial performance
  • Optimize your status change usage

If you want to change your acquire-to-decommission process as part of a SAP S/4HANA transformation, the following dashboards might interest you. Please consider that they might also prove useful as part of continuous business process improvement initiatives.

  • Analyze and improve your Best-Run Score
  • Harmonize your process

The data, as customary with the plug-and-gain approach, is sourced from SAP Signavio Process Insights. This allows to quickly provide data without building or leveraging dedicated extractors in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. It is a combination of different process flows and the performance indicator for status changes to plant maintenance orders. Based on this, a holistic view of the acquire-to-decommission process can be provided.

The dashboards are structured to seamlessly lead users through the analyses, providing explanations of context and motivation, along with potential conclusions for each analysis. Additionally, users will receive recommendations on how to proceed and address the root causes of process issues. This facilitates business users in navigating the analysis without requiring extensive technical knowledge of SAP ERP or expertise in process mining.

As part of this blog, we will focus on a couple of sample use cases showcasing how the content can be used to improve your acquire-to-decommission process.

Example: Avoid corrective maintenance

Start out by analyzing your share of preventive and corrective maintenance. A high share of corrective maintenance, which typically occurs ad hoc and unplanned, indicates that you might need to invest more in preventive maintenance, which helps you avoid situations in which you must react at short notice to avoid business disruptions.

Subsequently, you might want to understand to which extent maintenance, be it preventive or corrective, is performed on time. If plant maintenance orders tend to be completed late, the plug and gain analysis for acquire-to-decommission can help you to understand root causes, such as overloaded work centers or specific equipment that are particularly prone to corrective maintenance.

This information can help you define counteractive measures such as distributing the workload between the different work centers, introducing a regular maintenance plan, or even exchanging equipment frequently requiring corrective maintenance.

The root cause analysis also considers master data and configuration issues, such as missing priority set. This impacts the planned start and end dates of maintenance orders and, therefore, the prioritization of maintenance orders.

Example: Analyze and improve your status usage

The status of a plant maintenance order controls which business activities can be performed on the plant maintenance order. That’s why it’s important to make sure the status is set as intended and to avoid setting and resetting the same status repeatedly. The sequence of status changes is partially controlled by SAP standards but heavily depends on your business context, especially when it comes to user status.

You can analyze typical patterns of status changes by using the variant analysis provided as part of the Value accelerator for acquire-to-decommission (plug and gain approach). By comparing this to your to-be process, you understand whether there is a need to work on as-is processes.

Moreover, the analysis provides you with information on to what extent status are revoked. This can be a sign of misalignment that might cause delays in the process.

Example: Optimize your financial performance

The acquire-to-decommission process has an impact not only on your production processes but also on your financial performance. If, for example, plant maintenance orders frequently take more effort and material than initially planned, you have to face cost overruns. Using the plug and gain analyses for acquire-to-decommission, you can identify plants and work centers for whose actual costs frequently exceed planned cost. As a prerequisite for such an analysis, planned costs need to be available on the plant maintenance order, which is also analyzed as part of the Optimize your financial performance dashboard.

Analyzing commitments along the lifecycle of plant maintenance orders, especially on plant maintenance orders with cost overrun, adds to the financial performance analysis provided. Settlement is an important process step for the acquire-to-decommission process since it represents the point in time when the plant maintenance order is debited and the costs are assigned to, for example, a cost center or are capitalized in the case of asset manufacturing. Understanding whether maintenance orders have been settled and whether they are settled frequently supports you in understanding whether you have to optimize your settlement process by, for example, making sure that technically completed plant maintenance orders are settled.

How to get it?

If you believe that the above above-mentioned analyses would offer value to you and your organization, you might be wondering how to obtain them. These analyses are included in the plug-and-gain approach by SAP Signavio, which has been launched at Sapphire. This approach harnesses the capabilities of the SAP Signavio Suite and offers distinctive out-of-the-box analyses for both continuous improvement and transformation projects. By leveraging this approach, you can accelerate lengthy implementation projects and quickly access valuable insights. Reach out to your SAP counterpart to learn more about how you could benefit from plug and gain by SAP Signavio!

Where to find further information

  • The following note explains the prerequisite and scope of plug and gain by SAP Signavio: https://me.sap.com/notes/3340986/E
  • Previous blogs on Best-Run scores, process house, transaction scores and process flow variants, invoice-to-pay and invoice-to-cash
  • Plug and gain approach: https://www.signavio.com/plug-and-gain-approach/

entry.title: Breaking Down Barriers: Hear from Chelcie Scott How She Broke into Tech with the Help of SAP
entry.content:
“Being a young parent is very hard”, Chelcie recounts. She was 20 when her daughter Eva was born, moving to Atlanta just one year later. Chelcie wanted to prioritize her little girl, but also needed to make a living as a single mother. She wanted her daughter to be proud of her. “I always wanted to be the example I wish I had”, she says. So, she moved to Atlanta, hoping for a better life.  

Starting to think bigger 

Chelcie soon started her own business as a performance coach. She still remembers the many highs and lows the challenge of being a single mother and entrepreneur all in one. “Having an income that’s not always reliable – it’s difficult. I’ve always enjoyed entrepreneurship, but it always comes with a certain cost of things.” She was in survival mode, as she says, and thinking bigger wasn’t in the cards for her as she tried to make ends meet. Until she was offered the chance to pivot into the tech industry.  

With the help from Rejoice Jones from Vower, an organisation which aims to connect communities with non-traditional employers, and SAP’s digital skills initiative, Chelcie joined an SAP S/4HANA bootcamp. “I want to change the trajectory for my family, to show my daughter that women, especially that black women have the capacity and skills to work in these spaces and sectors”, she says, aware of the unshakably low figures. After all, with only 27% of all computing-related jobs held by women, and the number for people from ethnic minorities dropping even lower, accessing digital skills and securing a fulfilling, well-paid job in the tech industry often seems out of reach for most of the world’s population.

Digital skills initiative provides free learning and certification 

“But Rejoice and SAP encouraged me to imagine and think bigger”, Chelcie explains. “I grew up in a low-income household. I was the first generation to go to college.” Knowing that you are just not exposed to what’s out there, you don’t always have the contacts and don’t always see people like you in those positions, she explains.

Designed to bridge the gap between providing easily accessible training and certifications for everyone considered underrepresented in the technology industry and supporting organizations with their workforce transformation and search for fresh talent, SAP’s digital skills initiative contributes to a more diverse and equitable job market, workforce, and society as a whole. “There are also so many barriers into these industries which is why the SAP free bootcamp and certification was so amazing for allowing me to take the next step”, Chelcie confirms.  

To learn more about Chelcie’s story, watch the video above – happy ending guaranteed!

Start your own success story today 

And if you want to follow in her footsteps, kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career. We are looking forward to many more great success stories! 

Are you looking to hire people like Chelcie? Reach out to the digital skills initiative for information on how to get in contact with her and other people like her who are entering the SAP ecosystem with a fresh cloud-first mindset. We are happy to connect any partner or customer with our candidates to provide a mutual growth opportunity for new talent and the organizations supporting our ecosystem.

entry.title: Develop a Side-by-Side CAP-Based Extension Application Following the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce a new Discovery Center mission Develop a Side-by-Side CAP-Based Extension Application Following the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide  which is a sequel to our previous mission Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application Following the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide and is available in  SAP Discovery Center.

The primary objective of this mission is to demonstrate the implementation of a side-by-side SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)-based extension application, following the recommendations outlined in the  SAP BTP Developer’s Guide . 

Through a step-by-step tutorial, one will learn how to develop a sample application called Incident Management, which serves as a practical learning tool. The Incident Management application is developed in accordance with best practices for building side-by-side extension applications using CAP and SAP Fiori.

Key feature showcased in this application include:

  • Development of a full-stack applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) 
  • Consuming events from SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Event Mesh 
  • Integration of the application with REST APIs from SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Side-by-Side Extensibility  
  • Deployment of the same application to both SAP BTP CF and SAP BTP Kyma runtime 

Business Scenario: 

In context of this mission, the Incident Management application assists a company called ACME in managing its  Customer Incidents.  ACME has call centre with support representatives(known as Processor) who handle customer incidents. When a customer reports an incident over the phone, a Processor creates a new incident on behalf of the customer. The newly created incident includes details of the conversation between the Processor and the customer. Furthermore, the Incident Management application enables support team members working on a customer's incident to provide updates in the form of conversations.

The scenario encompasses the following aspects: 

  • Development of a full stack custom extension application that operates independently from SAP S/4HANA Cloud application
  • Securely connectivity to SAP S/4 HANA  Cloud API’s 
  • Real-time communication of changes in SAP S/4HANA objects to extension application via events

OData Mock Server 

 Even if you do not have access to SAP S/4HANA Cloud system you can still try out our mission using the OData mock server as the source of API and events. It contains entities of SAP OData services with sample data required to run the mission 

The Discovery Centre Mission project board provides step-by-step guidance through which you will get a complete picture of how the end-to-end use case works. By trying out the use case you will also get a flavour of the different technologies showcased in this mission.  

Navigate to Option1: Cloud Foundry Runtime or Option 3: Kyma Runtime and Mock Server to access the tutorials for running the mission using OData mock server application. 

          

Solution Diagram 

           

Useful Links: 

  • Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application Following the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide 
  • For additional tutorials refer SAP Samples GitHub repository 
  • What is SAP BTP Developer’s Guide
